data.title: Adversarial review of inference-sourced decisions + unresolved-questions forcing function
data.description:
## Context

Sibling epic to the preconditions manifest epic. Where the manifest epic creates the schema + validators + stage-boundary enforcement, this epic addresses two orthogonal concerns: (1) systematically challenging inference-sourced decisions that affect high-blast-radius manifest fields, and (2) ensuring brainstorm exhausts user intent while user attention is available rather than deferring question discovery to later phases.

Both mechanisms consume the decisions_log and unresolved_questions fields defined by the preconditions manifest epic (SC2 standard/deep tiers) but do not require the manifest schema to exist for their own adversarial-review prompts to be designed. This separation allows the review mechanism to be iterated independently of the manifest rollout.

## Success Criteria

- SC1: Brainstorm Phase 1 gate requires emitting a completeness attestation (exhausted | open) before spec exit. 'Open' attestations must list each unresolved question with a blocking_for phase; brainstorm cannot exit with questions blocking brainstorm itself. Mid-workflow question discovery routes back via existing REPLAN_ESCALATE signal.
- SC2: dso:red-team-reviewer extended with a new inference-challenge prompt mode. Inference-sourced decisions in the preconditions manifest's decisions_log field are challenged at tiered rates: 100% review for decisions where affects_fields includes gate_verdicts or workflow_completion_checklist; 20% sample (deterministic by hash(decision_id) mod 5 == 0) for rationale-text-only inferences.
- SC3: Review challenges surface as ticket comments with structured output: {decision_id, challenge_type, evidence_against_inference, user_confirmation_required: bool}. No new sub-agent dispatched; existing red-team-reviewer handles the new prompt mode.
- SC4: Coverage measurement: dry-run replay of 818-bug historical corpus shows inference challenges would have surfaced ≥20 of the 100 bugs caught by the broader manifest program (the subset where an inference-sourced decision later proved incorrect).
- SC5: The sampling rate config (preconditions.inference_review.rate_rationale, preconditions.inference_review.rate_high_stakes) is tunable without code changes.

## Dependencies

- Depends on: the preconditions manifest epic (manifest schema defines decisions_log and its affects_fields attribute; without the schema, this epic's challenge targets don't exist)
- Depends on: existing dso:red-team-reviewer
- Relates to: ca48-c3c3 (Enrich Planning Pipeline Context) — SC1 extends brainstorm context completeness

## Out of Scope

- The preconditions manifest schema itself (separate epic)
- Stage-boundary validators (separate epic)
- Non-inference decision auditing (user-sourced decisions still checked via evidence source_ref keyword check, but not adversarially challenged)
